description: Verified subset of manifest.json. 23 bugs of 44 candidates (52%) survived Phase-0 install-verification per DESIGN.md §13.4. Drops follow Bohme et al. ICSE'22 discipline — see dropped.json for per-bug reasons.

anchor.verification_rule: PR #1554 merged 2021-06-10, first in htsjdk 3.0.0 on 2022-06-06 (AC/AN/AF filtered genotypes)

trigger.description: AC/AN/AF allele counts include filtered genotypes marked FT; should exclude filtered variants.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 3.0.4 (2022-11-23) hotfix reverted PR #1593 which had caused #1637's VCF sort-order regression

trigger.description: VCF sort order changed to use alleles for stability; breaks merging of existing valid VCFs causing false 'unsorted' errors.

anchor.verification_rule: Issue #1314 filed 2024-10-21 against 0.22.1; fix expected in 0.23.0 development stream

trigger.description: VariantFile.write() maps records to wrong contig when header contigs manually edited.

anchor.verification_rule: pysam 0.23.0 release notes explicitly cite 'VariantHeader.new_record GT setting (#1308)'

trigger.description: VariantHeader.new_record() fails to set GT field on second+ record creation; KeyError: 'invalid FORMAT: GT'.

anchor.verification_rule: PR #4837 merged 2024-09-23 (SAM deepcopy perf); first release after 1.85 is 1.86

trigger.description: Excessive copy.deepcopy() in SAM parser; perf degradation with potential correctness impact on large files.
trigger.notes: Primarily performance; correctness impact secondary. May be dropped.

anchor.verification_rule: git rev-parse confirmed: PR #2418 merge commit + parent

trigger.description: BAM parsing forgets to consume sequence bytes when creating dummy alignments; stream iterator misaligned, corrupts subsequent record reads.
trigger.notes: Trigger file test.bam.gz present in original issue #2417.

anchor.verification_rule: git rev-parse confirmed: PR #3081 merge commit + parent

trigger.description: Empty SAM/BAM output files invalid (no header written); file unusable unless records explicitly written.

anchor.verification_rule: git rev-parse confirmed: PR #3269 merge commit + parent

trigger.description: Banded alignment returns relative positions of sliced sequences instead of absolute positions of originals; off-by-prefix offset.

anchor.verification_rule: git rev-parse confirmed: PR #3098 merge commit + parent

trigger.description: Alignment score calculation wrong in traceback; incorrect carry bit tracking on up/left open directions causes wrong score.

anchor.verification_rule: git log --merges found merge commit edbfa956f for PR #2869 (correct_id_parsing_fasta)

trigger.description: FASTA parser fails on IDs containing '>' character; treats '> >MyID' as ID 'MyID' instead of '>MyID'.
trigger.notes: FASTA-adjacent — may be out of SAM scope; evaluate during pre-flight.

anchor.verification_rule: git rev-parse confirmed: PR #3406 merge commit + parent

trigger.description: BGZF stream data race condition in concurrent reads; non-deterministic corruption.
trigger.notes: Data race — requires multithreaded harness; may be hard to trigger reliably in 2h.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 2.20.0 release notes cite 'Tolerate mixed case NaNs and Infinities in VCF (#1364)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix VCF codec rejects QUAL / INFO float values spelled with non-lowercase 'nan' or 'inf' (e.g. 'NaN', 'Inf', 'Infinity'), which htslib accepts per the VCF spec's float production rule. Causes differential disagreement on any VCF where upstream tools emit mixed-case float literals.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 2.20.0 release notes cite 'Output missing vcf fields as a single . (#1389)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix VCF writer serialises multi-value missing fields as '.,.,.' (one dot per value) instead of the spec-canonical single '.' for fully-missing values. Round-trip through pre-fix htsjdk changes INFO/FORMAT string representation.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 2.20.0 release notes cite 'VCF codec should handle multiple missing GL fields (#1372)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix VCF codec fails to handle FORMAT=GL when multiple per-genotype GL values are each individually missing ('.'). Throws instead of producing null, even though htslib accepts the input.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 2.20.0 release notes cite 'Handle PEDIGREE header lines differently for vcf4.2 vs vcf4.3 (#1401)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix VCF header parser produces inconsistent PEDIGREE entries between VCF 4.2 and 4.3 inputs; the same semantic pedigree record round-trips differently depending on declared fileformat version.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 2.20.1 release notes cite 'fix bug in VariantContextBuilder (#1403)' — explicitly a hotfix for the 2.20.0 regression

trigger.description: Regression in VariantContextBuilder introduced in htsjdk 2.20.0 — the builder emits incorrect field values under certain chains; hotfix in 2.20.1 reverts the regression.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 2.21.0 release notes cite 'Make VCFHeader not throw exception if contig header lines lack length field (#1418)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix VCF header parser throws on any '##contig=<ID=X>' header line that omits the length= attribute, even though the spec treats length as optional. htslib accepts such files; pre-fix htsjdk rejects them.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 3.0.0 release notes cite 'Added GVCF mode for VariantContext type determination (#1544)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix VariantContext.getType() mis-classifies gVCF-style <NON_REF> records, producing wrong variant-type labels for gVCF blocks. Downstream tools that filter by variant type therefore miss entire classes of records.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 3.0.0 release notes cite 'Validate that SAM header tag keys are exactly 2 characters long (#1561)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix SAM header parser silently accepts @HD / @SQ / @RG / @PG lines with tag keys longer or shorter than 2 characters (SAM spec §1.3 mandates exactly 2). Malformed headers pass through pre-fix and produce SAMFileHeader objects with garbage-keyed attributes.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 2.24.1 release notes cite 'Invaliding out of date SAMRecord.mAlignmentBlocks cache (#1538)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix SAMRecord mutation (e.g. setCigar) does not invalidate the cached mAlignmentBlocks vector. Subsequent calls to getAlignmentBlocks() on the mutated record return stale data from before the CIGAR edit. Silent wrong answer on any mutation-followed-by-query pattern.

anchor.verification_rule: htsjdk 2.23.0 release notes cite 'Fixed a bug in the locus accumulator where the accumulator did not add... (#1489)'

trigger.description: Pre-fix SAM locus accumulator drops certain insertion events, under-counting coverage at insert positions. Downstream pileup statistics computed via htsjdk disagree with samtools mpileup at these sites.

anchor.verification_rule: pysam 0.22.0 NEWS cites #1214 as fixed in the AlignmentFile cleanup

trigger.description: Pre-fix AlignmentFile iteration over certain malformed-but-spec-tolerated SAM files produces records with incorrect per-record fields. Fixed together with #939 as part of a broader AlignmentFile robustness pass in 0.22.0.

anchor.verification_rule: pysam 0.22.0 NEWS cites #939 as fixed in the AlignmentFile cleanup

trigger.description: Long-standing AlignmentFile bug in pysam <=0.21; the 0.22.0 release bundled its fix together with #1214. Specific symptom deferred to trigger-folder research.
